description: Core Gameplay — Swimming, Cat Companions & Resource Gathering. Adds swimming/diving with oxygen, 4 cat companion types (Loaf, Zoomies, Curiosity Cat, Pounce), resource nodes with gathering, inventory system, and yarn economy.

userStories.title: SwimmingState component and water trigger volumes
userStories.description: As a player, I want to swim when I enter water so that water areas feel like a distinct environment.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ecs/components/SwimmingState.ts component with fields: isSwimming (bool), isDiving (bool), oxygen (number 0-100), currentDepth (number)
Water zones from MapData are created as trigger volumes in PhysicsEngine during MapManager.loadMap()
When player entity overlaps a water trigger: SwimmingState component added automatically, PLAYER_ENTERED_WATER event emitted via EventBus
When player exits water: SwimmingState component removed, PLAYER_EXITED_WATER event emitted
Water zones on test map render as semi-transparent blue planes
Transition is seamless — no teleporting or state glitches at water boundary
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Swimming movement — surface swim, dive, ascend
userStories.description: As a player, I want to swim on the surface and dive underwater with distinct controls.
acceptanceCriteria: MovementSystem detects SwimmingState component on player entity and switches to swim physics
Surface swim: 360deg movement at 3.2 u/s (from config.ts swimming.surfaceSpeed)
Dive: S-key moves player downward at 2.0 u/s
Ascend: W-key or Spacebar moves player upward at 2.5 u/s
Player cannot jump while swimming (Spacebar triggers ascend instead)
Player surfaces automatically when reaching water edge boundary
Water zones have configurable depth (depth property in MapData waterZone)
Movement feels floaty compared to ground (reduced acceleration curve)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Oxygen system with HUD gauge and health drain
userStories.description: As a player, I want an oxygen meter when diving so that underwater exploration has tension and stakes.
acceptanceCriteria: Oxygen starts at 100 when submerging, drains at 3.33 per second (1% per 0.3s) while isDiving is true
Oxygen does NOT drain while on water surface (only when isDiving = true)
HUD oxygen gauge: bar element appears at top-center when SwimmingState is present, hidden on land
Warning: pulsing red visual on gauge at 20% oxygen remaining, OXYGEN_WARNING event emitted
Depletion: when oxygen hits 0, player loses 1 health per second, OXYGEN_DEPLETED event emitted
Surfacing: oxygen refills at 5% per second when on water surface (isDiving = false)
Exiting water completely: oxygen resets to 100, gauge hides
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Cat definition data structure and registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a data-driven cat definition system so that adding new cat types requires only a definition file and enum entry.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/cats/CatDefinition.ts defines CatDefinition interface with: type (CatType enum), yarnCost (number), name (string), description (string), behavior config (duration, cooldown), meshConfig (shape, color, size for primitive), effectType ('terrain' | 'movement' | 'utility' | 'launch')
src/game/cats/definitions/ directory created with one file per cat type
src/game/cats/definitions/index.ts barrel exports a registry Map<CatType, CatDefinition>
Registry is iterable and lookup by CatType is O(1)
Placeholder definitions created for all 4 MVP cats (Loaf, Zoomies, CuriosityCat, Pounce) with correct yarn costs and effect types
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CatBehavior component and CatAISystem
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a generic system that drives all cat behaviors from their definitions so that cat behavior is data-driven.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ecs/components/CatBehavior.ts component with: catType (CatType), state (Idle | Active | Cooldown | Expired), stateTimer (number), ownerId (Entity reference)
src/systems/CatAISystem.ts implements System interface
Each frame: queries entities with CatBehavior + Transform
State machine per cat: Idle (just placed) -> Active (effect running) -> Expired (duration elapsed)
Terrain cats (effectType terrain/launch): stay in Active indefinitely until manually dismissed
Duration cats (effectType movement/utility): timer counts down from definition duration, auto-transition to Expired
When state is Expired: system removes the cat entity and emits CAT_DISMISSED event
System reads behavior config from CatDefinition registry — no cat-specific logic hardcoded
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CatCompanionManager — summon, dismiss, catalog
userStories.description: As a player, I want to summon and dismiss cat companions using yarn so that I can solve puzzles and traverse the world.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/CatCompanionManager.ts exposes: summon(catType, position): Entity | null, dismiss(entity), getCatalog(): CatCatalogEntry[], getActiveCompanions(): Entity[]
summon() validates: enough yarn (checks GameState player.yarn), under cat limit (3 active), position is valid (not inside static collider geometry)
On valid summon: deducts yarn from GameState, creates cat entity with Transform, Renderable, Collider, CatBehavior components, emits CAT_SUMMONED event
On invalid summon: returns null (no entity created)
dismiss(): destroys cat entity, returns yarn to GameState, emits CAT_DISMISSED event
getCatalog(): returns all cat definitions with type, name, yarnCost, description
Cat limit: if summoning would exceed limit of 3, oldest cat is auto-dismissed first
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Loaf cat — static platform block
userStories.description: As a player, I want to summon a Loaf cat that becomes a solid platform so I can reach elevated areas by stacking.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/cats/definitions/Loaf.ts CatDefinition: type=Loaf, yarnCost=1, effectType='terrain', meshConfig with box geometry at 1.5u height
On summon: cat entity created with static Collider (isStatic=true, box shape, 1.5u tall)
Player can walk on top of the Loaf entity (collision response supports standing on static bodies)
Multiple Loafs stackable — player can place one atop another's top face
Loaf is immovable (static body — nothing pushes it)
Visual: box-shaped colored primitive (warm orange color distinct from terrain)
On dismiss: collider removed, entity destroyed — player falls if standing on it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Zoomies cat — speed trail with timed expiry
userStories.description: As a player, I want to summon a Zoomies cat that creates a speed trail so I can move faster through an area.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/cats/definitions/Zoomies.ts CatDefinition: type=Zoomies, yarnCost=2, effectType='movement', duration=8 seconds, behavior.speedMultiplier=2.0
On summon: cat entity spawns at target position and creates a trigger zone (elongated box collider, 6u long) extending in player's current facing direction
When player overlaps the trail trigger: movement speed multiplied by 2x (MovementSystem checks for speed buff triggers)
Speed buff applies only while player is inside the trail zone
Trail persists for 8 seconds then auto-expires (CatAISystem handles via duration timer)
Visual: elongated colored zone (bright yellow/green) showing the trail path
On auto-expire: yarn is consumed (not returned). On manual right-click dismiss before expiry: yarn returned
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Curiosity Cat — reveal hidden terrain
userStories.description: As a player, I want to summon a Curiosity Cat that reveals hidden terrain so I can discover secret paths and areas.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/cats/definitions/CuriosityCat.ts CatDefinition: type=CuriosityCat, yarnCost=2, effectType='utility', duration=20 seconds, behavior.revealRadius=5
Hidden terrain zones from MapData are created as entities with Renderable (initially invisible/opacity=0) and Collider (initially disabled/isTrigger=true)
On summon: all hidden terrain entities within 5u radius of cat position become visible (opacity fades to 1) and their colliders switch to solid (isTrigger=false, player can walk on them)
Revealed terrain persists for 20 seconds then fades back to hidden (opacity back to 0, collider back to trigger)
HIDDEN_TERRAIN_REVEALED event emitted with list of affected terrain entity IDs
Cat remains at summon position during reveal duration
Auto-expires after 20s (yarn consumed). Manual dismiss before expiry returns yarn
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pounce cat — upward launch pad
userStories.description: As a player, I want to summon a Pounce cat that launches me upward so I can reach high platforms in one move.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/cats/definitions/Pounce.ts CatDefinition: type=Pounce, yarnCost=3, effectType='launch', behavior.launchImpulse=3.5
On summon: cat entity created with trigger collider on top surface
When player walks onto the Pounce cat trigger: applies 3.5u upward impulse to player Velocity.dy
Launch occurs once per landing — a cooldown flag resets when player touches ground elsewhere (prevents infinite bouncing)
Pounce cat persists until manually dismissed (effectType='launch' treated as persistent like terrain)
Right-click dismiss returns yarn
Visual: low wide box primitive (crouching shape) with distinct purple/magenta color
Player retains 70% air control during launch (same as normal jump physics)
Test map elevated platforms at 3.5u height reachable via Pounce
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cat placement UI — ghost preview, selection, dismiss
userStories.description: As a player, I want to aim and place cats with my mouse so that positioning is intuitive and precise.
acceptanceCriteria: Number keys 1-4 select cat type from roster (updates active selection state)
When cat type selected: ghost preview mesh (semi-transparent, 50% opacity) shown at mouse world position via SceneManager
Ghost preview snaps to valid ground positions (raycasts down from mouse position)
Ghost changes to red color when position is invalid (inside wall, off map, inside existing collider)
Left-click at valid position: calls CatCompanionManager.summon() with selected type and position
Right-click on an active cat entity: calls CatCompanionManager.dismiss() for that cat
HUD cat selection bar: shows all 4 cat types with icons, yarn costs, and current yarn count. Selected type highlighted
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: ResourceNode component and gathering system
userStories.description: As a player, I want to gather resources from nodes on the map so that I feel productive and have materials to progress.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ecs/components/ResourceNode.ts component: resourceType (Grass|Sticks|Water), gatherTime (seconds), yieldAmount (number), respawnTime (seconds), cooldownRemaining (number), isBeingGathered (bool)
Resource node entities have Transform, Renderable (distinct shape/color per type), Collider (trigger zone for interaction range ~1.5u), ResourceNode component
Player presses E while inside a resource node trigger zone: gathering starts if node cooldownRemaining is 0
Gathering takes gatherTime seconds (Grass 1.5s, Sticks 1.5s, Water 2.0s from config/GDD)
Player moving during gather cancels it (no penalty, progress resets)
Gather complete: resource added to inventory (GameState), node enters cooldown, visual dims/fades
After cooldown expires: node becomes gatherable again, visual restores to full opacity
Test map spawns nodes from MapData spawn points: 8-12 Grass, 3-5 Sticks, 1-2 Water
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inventory system with capacity limits
userStories.description: As a player, I want an inventory that holds gathered resources with a capacity limit so that I make strategic choices about what to carry.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState tracks player.inventory as array of {resourceType, quantity} stacks and player.inventory.maxCapacity (default 10)
When gathering: if total items across all stacks < maxCapacity, add resource. If full, gathering fails with feedback
Resources of same type stack (e.g., 5 Grass is one stack entry with quantity 5, not 5 separate entries)
Capacity counts total items across all stacks (10 Grass + 5 Sticks = 15 items, exceeds cap of 10)
HUD inventory display: shows resource type icons with quantities and capacity indicator (e.g., '7/10')
GameState.onChange('player.inventory') triggers HUD update reactively
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Yarn economy, HUD display, and yarn pickups
userStories.description: As a player, I need a yarn counter in the HUD and yarn pickups on the map so I can summon cats.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState tracks player.yarn as number (starts at 10 for testing)
Summoning a cat deducts yarnCost from player.yarn; dismissing adds it back
HUD shows yarn count with a yarn ball icon (colored circle) at top of screen
When yarn insufficient for selected cat type: ghost preview shows red and summon is blocked
Yarn count updates reactively via GameState.onChange('player.yarn')
Yarn pickup entities on test map: trigger collider, when player overlaps: adds yarn amount to player.yarn, destroys pickup entity
2-3 yarn pickups on test map giving +3 yarn each
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Enhanced HUD — complete status display
userStories.description: As a player, I need a complete HUD showing health, yarn, inventory, oxygen, active cats, and gather progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Health display: 5 hearts (filled/empty based on GameState player.stats.health)
Yarn count: yarn ball icon + number (already from US-014, ensure consistent styling)
Inventory: resource type icons with quantities + capacity bar (already from US-013, ensure consistent styling)
Oxygen gauge: appears ONLY when SwimmingState component present, hides on land
Active cat bar: shows small icons of summoned cats in bottom-left with right-click dismiss hint text
Cat selection: 4 cat type icons with yarn costs in bottom-center, selected type has highlighted border
Gather progress bar: appears during gathering near the resource node, smooth fill, disappears on complete/cancel
All HUD elements positioned at screen edges, not obstructing center gameplay
All elements update reactively via GameState.onChange()
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
